steps.title: Identify the vivid image illustrating the claim
steps.content: Bartley states a general claim about his bad luck (“If there's ever any misfortune coming to this world, it's on myself it pitches”), then immediately uses a vivid, concrete image (“like a flock of crows on seed potatoes”) to make it more tangible.

steps.title: Recognize the image's emphatic function
steps.content: The vivid simile emphasizes Bartley's claim by making it concrete and memorable. A, B, and D each misidentify it as an observation, counterexample, or skepticism instead of figurative language. That matches choice C — C is correct.
